text: Three questions to ace your next Genii interview.
Week 2, Question 1.
This week's topic, Transformers.
Question.
Explain the self-attention mechanism in Transformer.
Answer.
Self-attention is core to Transformers,
enabling them to weigh the importance of other elements in the same sentence.
Instead of processing words one by one like RNNs,
Self-attention looks at the entire sequence all at once.
For each word, it calculates scores against all other words using something called learned
query key and value vector.
These scores, normalized by softmax, determine how much each word contributes to the current
word's representation.
This captures long range dependencies effortlessly regardless of distance and is highly highly
parallelizable.
Pro tip, mention multi-headed attention.
Explain it runs multiple self-attention calculations in parallel using different query key value
splits.
This lets the model capture diverse relationships simultaneously.
self-attention and you will stand out.
Facing more JNI questions?
We will answer more in the next episode in this series.
